name: T-Rex
description: Carnivore that lived in North America.
discovery_info: T. rex was first discovered in 1902 by Barnum Brown in the Hell Creek Formation of Montana.
palaeobiology_info: T. rex had massive jaw strength and was built for hunting large herbivorous dinosaurs. Its keen senses made it a formidable hunter.

name: Spinosaurus
description: Pescivore that lived in Northern Africa.
discovery_info: Spinosaurus fossils were first described in 1915 by Ernst Stromer.
palaeobiology_info: The only known semi-aquatic dinosaur. Its large dorsal sail may have been used for display or thermoregulation.

name: Stegosaurus
description: Herbivore that lived in the United States and Portugal.
discovery_info: Stegosaurus was first discovered in 1877 in the Morrison Formation of Colorado.
palaeobiology_info: Stegosaurus’s plates were likely for display or thermoregulation, while the thagomizer was for defense.

name: Triceratops
description: Herbivore that lived in North America.
discovery_info: Triceratops was first discovered in 1887 by Othniel Charles Marsh in Denver, Colorado.
palaeobiology_info: A large bony frill protecting its neck and three large horns used in defense and social displays.

name: Ankylosaurus
description: Herbivore that lived in North America.
discovery_info: Ankylosaurus was first discovered in 1906 by Peter Kaisen during an expedition from the American Museum of Natural History.
palaeobiology_info: One of the most heavily armored dinosaurs with extensive bony plating covering its body and a club-like tail.

name: Carnotaurus
description: Carnivore that lived in South America.
discovery_info: Carnotaurus was first described in 1985 after being discovered in Argentina.
palaeobiology_info: Distinctive horns above its eyes. Its robust build and strong legs made it an effective hunter.

name: Brachiosaurus
description: Herbivore that lived in North America.
discovery_info: Brachiosaurus was first discovered in 1900 in the Morrison Formation by Elmer S. Riggs.
palaeobiology_info: One of the largest land animals ever, with a long neck reaching high into trees for vegetation.

name: Mosasaurus
description: Carnivore that lived in the Atlantic Ocean.
discovery_info: Mosasaurus fossils have been discovered across marine deposits worldwide.
palaeobiology_info: Mosasaurus was an apex marine predator with powerful jaws, sharp teeth, and a muscular tail.

name: Woolly Mammoth
description: Herbivore that lived in North America, Asia, and Europe.
discovery_info: Woolly mammoths were described in in 1799 by Johann Friedrich Blumenbach.
palaeobiology_info: Woolly mammoths were adapted to cold climates with thick fur, small ears, and a layer of fat for insulation.

name: Pteranodon
description: Pescavore that lived in North America.
discovery_info: Pteronadon was described in 1876 by Othniel Charles Marsh.
palaeobiology_info: It had a distinctive head crest and hunted fish from the air, using its long pointed beak to snatch prey from the sea.
